rules: Claim substantial lanes before editing shared docs.
Append events and worklog notes; do not overwrite another agent's entries.
Treat Agent-Worklog.md as append-only history; append-order supersessions and Current-Source-Status-Snapshot.md beat stale timestamped notes.
Use source-backed evidence for broken/partial feature claims.

Keep docs/wiki and the GitHub wiki checkout reconciliation explicit before publishing; current checkout validation is docs/validate-wiki.ps1 plus git diff --check, JSON/JSONL parsing when touched, and SHA256 parity over mirrored files. The old Tools/ValidateWiki.ps1 and Tools/TestWikiParity.ps1 helper names are historical unless those scripts are restored.
Treat external reports as leads until repo evidence confirms them.
Use ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md before patching RequestSpecial "ICBM", NukeIncoming, NukeDammage or ICBM Tactical UI/marker flow.
Use External-Integrations.md BattlEye Filter before claiming shipped BattlEye/public-server hardening; it owns DR-30 posture evidence and production BEpath caveats.
For attack-wave authority work, use Attack-Wave-Authority-Playbook.md and preserve the current all-side-supply spend model unless the owner approves a design change.

For headless-client work, use HC-Upstream-History-And-Lessons.md plus AI-Headless-And-Performance.md before changing routing, side-targeted PVF behavior, static-defense update-back, HC slot names or generated mission HC slots.

ownerSplit.Codex: Navigation, atlas integration, machine-readable context, docs/wiki mirror and publish validation.
ownerSplit.Codex-2: Scoped OA command-reference compatibility addenda when explicitly claimed; avoid overlapping those command-reference files until the active claim signs off.
ownerSplit.Claude: Autonomous source-backed deep reviews, contradiction hunting and subsystem archaeology.

openLanes.nextAction: Continue bounded source-backed wiki upkeep, compact dashboard/status surfaces, validate and publish scoped batches.
No further dashboard spam cleanup blocker remains; keep future historic batch detail in Agent-Worklog.md, Subagent-Discovery-Swarm.md and agent-events.jsonl.
Object lifecycle command guardrails are now folded into Arma-2-OA-Command-Version-Reference.md; no active Codex-2 claim remains.
Self-select the next bounded source-backed review from Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md or the hardening backlog.
Fold newly confirmed findings into Feature Status, owner pages and machine records when evidence appears.
Future code owners should claim implementation work from agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl only when gameplay patches are in scope.
Use Testing-Debugging-And-Release-Workflow.md and agent-test-plan.schema.json for future gameplay patch validation records.
Smoke client skill init, hosted FPS, supply scan and paratrooper marker lanes before release-complete claims.
Patch and smoke the bounded WASP display wait cleanup when code work is claimed.

pr1SupplyHeliContext.branchScope: PR #1 / feat/supply-helicopter is in scope for docs; master remains the stable baseline.
pr1SupplyHeliContext.summary: PR #1 adds supply helicopters, cash runs and interdiction rewards on top of the existing supply mission trust model.
knownRisks: Supply mission start/completion still trusts vehicle object variables set by client-started flow.
Cooldown casing mismatch LastSupplyMissionRun vs lastSupplyMissionRun remains unresolved.
Supply vehicle Killed event handlers can stack on reused vehicles; current double-payment is bounded but the handler leak is real.
Autonomous AI supply heli work is deferred because the underlying AI supply logistics path is already incomplete/broken.

rules: Use Bohemia Interactive Arma 2 OA scripting docs; avoid Arma 3 assumptions.

Use Arma-2-OA-Compatibility-Audit.md before accepting modern SQF or networking examples; avoid remoteExec/remoteExecCall, remoteExecutedOwner, isRemoteExecuted, allPlayers, SQF params, isEqualTo, private _var = value, parseSimpleArray, setGroupOwner, groupOwner, modern select range/filter forms, BIS_fnc_MP and CBA/ACE helpers unless OA/source proof exists.
Gameplay mission edits belong in the Chernarus source mission.
Run Tools/LoadoutManager dotnet run after mission edits to propagate generated missions.
Treat missing 7za as packaging-only unless deployment packaging is required.
Use WF_Debug-gated detailed logs and small always-on INFORMATION/WARNING logs for tester-visible state transitions.
Use PowerShell -LiteralPath for mission paths containing [55-2hc].
Record validation level for gameplay changes with Testing-Debugging-And-Release-Workflow.md and agent-test-plan.schema.json; source-only review is not in-game smoke proof.

Use Arma-2-OA-External-Reference-Guide.md before accepting engine-level claims about public variables, PVEHs, setVariable publication, event handlers, nearestObjects, JIP, marker timing or performance diagnostics.
Treat publicVariable and setVariable [..., true] as replicated last-value state only; they do not prove server authority, event-history replay or full marker/queue collection sync.
Treat publicVariableClient as OA/source-backed targeted delivery only; unlike publicVariable, it is not persistent/JIP-compatible and needs a resend or request/response path for late-join state.
For server-authority or economy patches, read Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md before changing handlers; client UI checks are affordance, not authority.
Use ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md before patching RequestSpecial "ICBM", NukeIncoming, NukeDammage or ICBM Tactical UI/marker flow.

Use External-Integrations.md BattlEye Filter before claiming shipped BattlEye/public-server hardening; Feature Status, Networking/PV, Hardening roadmap and Server authority map should route there instead of repeating DR-30 evidence.
DR-41 rule: direct publicVariableServer channels such as ATTACK_WAVE_INIT require per-channel server recomputation; PVF dispatcher hardening alone does not cover them.
Attack-wave authority patches should read Attack-Wave-Authority-Playbook.md first; default behavior preserves all-current-side-supply spend, with 25000 as the minimum gate.
Use Economy-Authority-First-Cut.md before economy hardening code work; patch side-supply negative clamp first, then migrate existing PVF spend handlers before attempting player-buy locality redesign.

Use Abandoned-Feature-Revival-Review.md before reviving dormant MASH/paratrooper/WASP/UAV/UI/modded-mission paths; source-check liveness first and prefer remove/annotate over re-enabling half-wired code.

Use Performance-Opportunity-Sweep.md before performance patches; prefer PVF dispatch lookup, hosted FPS loop sleep, Skill_Init idempotency and supply scan narrowing before speculative marker/cleaner cadence changes.

Construction authority docs have split ownership: Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-6 owns exact RequestStructure/RequestDefense/RequestMHQRepair proof; Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md owns runtime flow, request-handler map and safe extension checklist; Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md owns migration design; Gameplay/Feature status/Roadmap should route there.

Supply-mission docs have split ownership: Supply-Mission-Architecture.md owns flow/state/JIP pattern; Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-18 owns exact cooldown casing defect evidence; Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook.md owns implementation-ready patch shape; Economy/Server runtime/Feature status/Roadmap should route there.

Generated-mission docs have split ownership: Tools-And-Build-Workflow.md owns operational LoadoutManager skip-list, packaging and status rules; Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-4/DR-32 own full drift evidence; Content-Structure-And-Maps.md only orients folder layout.

HC delegation docs have split ownership: AI-Headless-And-Performance.md routes runtime/source anchors, Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md owns HC boot timing, and Headless-Delegation-And-Failover-Playbook.md owns DR-21/DR-42 patch policy.

Lifecycle docs have split ownership: Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md owns flags, boot ordering, JIP waits and HC wait hazards; Mission-Entrypoints-And-Lifecycle.md owns include graph and role-dispatch orientation; SQF-Code-Atlas.md owns compile/source ownership; Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md owns long-running server loops.

Hardening authority docs have split ownership: Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md owns patch order/branch discipline/validation gates, while Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md owns authority design principles, flow table and handler checklist.

Use Current-Source-Status-Snapshot.md and agent-knowledge.jsonl#source-status-branch-matrix-2026-06-03 before trusting older worklog/event/knowledge entries that claim cleanup lanes already shipped. As of the 2026-06-03 branch matrix, docs/source carries paratrooper marker registration, duplicate Skill_Init removal, hosted FPS loop exits and supply scan narrowing in Chernarus plus maintained Vanilla; stable master does not, and release a9219d88 is partial for FPS/supply and absent for paratrooper/Skill_Init. Commander DR-15 and factory DR-33 remain current-code-change-needed, and the WASP display-54 wait remains an open cleanup opportunity.
Worklog/event timestamps can be non-monotonic; append-order supersession notes plus Current-Source-Status-Snapshot.md beat older or later-looking stale timestamps.

systems.networking: PVF lists in Common/Init/Init_PublicVariables.sqf plus direct publicVariable channels for attack waves, side supply, supply missions, MASH markers, HQ state, anti-stack compensation, day/night, FPS, AFK and markers. See Networking-And-Public-Variables.md.
systems.economy: Funds and side supply with town SV, commander income, resources FSM and supply missions. See Gameplay-Systems-Atlas for source-backed town/economy/commander/factory flow.
systems.construction: Commander/repair-truck CoIn flow, structure arrays, HQ deploy/mobilize, server construction workers, defense building, repair and sale flows. Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md owns the runtime map/checklist; Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-6 owns exact authority proof; Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md owns migration design.
systems.ai: Server town AI, AI commanders, client/headless delegation, player AI watchdog/recovery. AI-Headless-And-Performance.md is the runtime/source router; Headless-Delegation-And-Failover-Playbook.md covers DR-21/DR-42 HC tracking, static-defense update-back and disconnect policy. The confirmed occupied town-AI vehicle despawn bug has an implementation playbook in Town-AI-Vehicle-Despawn-Safety.md.
systems.ui: Rsc resources, Client/GUI dialog controllers, RHUD/FPS HUD, title resources, map/action marker loops, CoIn construction UI and WASP overlays. See Client-UI-Systems-Atlas.md.
systems.gearLoadoutEasa: Gear metadata/profile templates and EASA aircraft loadouts; generated EASA/balance output comes from LoadoutManager. See Gear-Loadout-And-EASA-Atlas.md.
systems.tooling: LoadoutManager for mission generation/packaging; PerformanceAuditAnalyzer for RPT metrics. Tools-And-Build-Workflow.md owns operational generation/skip-list rules, while DR-4/DR-32 own full generated-mission drift evidence.
systems.integrations: DiscordBot, in-repo a2waspwarfare_Extension/GLOBALGAMESTATS, out-of-repo A2WaspDatabase AntiStack extension, BattlEye publicVariable filter, custom license and missing CI posture. External-Integrations.md owns the canonical shipped BattlEye posture and DR-30 production BEpath caveat.
systems.waspOverlay: Project-specific WASP additions include base repair, RPG dropping, marker monitor, HQ recovery action, start vehicles and a server selftest. See WASP-Overlay.md.
systems.factoryPurchase: Player buy-unit flow is client-local from GUI_Menu_BuyUnits.sqf to Client_BuildUnit.sqf; RequestBuyUnit is not registered and no Server/PVFunctions/RequestBuyUnit.sqf exists. Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit is compiled but no static caller was found. See Factory-And-Purchase-Systems-Atlas.md.
systems.serverRuntime: Server gameplay loops are owned by Init_Server.sqf: town capture/SV, town AI activation/despawn, camp manager, resources/economy, victory checks, garbage/vehicle cleanup, server FPS and AntiStack loops. See Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md; victory/endgame correctness detail stays canonical in Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-11/DR-36.
systems.externalResearch: Three Steff-provided Dutch PDF reports and nine Markdown deep-research reports are summarized in external-research-report-manifest.json. Raw report text is not mirrored into the wiki. Treat all external-report claims as leads until repo evidence confirms them; Claude found earlier report citations were downstream corroboration from the wiki/upstream proxy. See External-Research-Reports.md.
systems.hardeningRoadmap: Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md plus agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl convert confirmed Auth/PV, correctness, performance and docs-polish findings into patch order, branch discipline and validation gates. Use Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md for authority design/checklist decisions, External-Integrations.md for shipped BattlEye posture and Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-11/DR-36 for victory/endgame mechanism detail. PVF-Dispatch-Implementation-Playbook.md covers DR-1/DR-38 dispatcher hardening; ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md covers DR-27 RequestSpecial/Nuke authority; Attack-Wave-Authority-Playbook.md covers confirmed-high-dr41 direct-PV authority.
systems.lifecycle: description.ext and initJIPCompatible.sqf are the entry front doors, but mission.sqm object init fields are also runtime producers: town logic objects call Init_Town.sqf and WF_Logic starts Init_TownMode.sqf. Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md owns boot flags/order/JIP/HC wait hazards; Mission-Entrypoints-And-Lifecycle.md owns include graph and role-dispatch orientation; SQF-Code-Atlas.md owns compile/source ownership; Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md owns long-running server loops.
systems.testing: Testing-Debugging-And-Release-Workflow.md defines source-only, local-tool, hosted/dedicated/JIP/HC smoke and live-server-sensitive validation levels. agent-test-plan.schema.json records machine-readable evidence so agents do not confuse source review with in-game proof.
systems.arma2OAExternalReferences: Arma-2-OA-External-Reference-Guide.md maps official BI OA scripting references to Wasp hotspots for multiplayer/JIP, public variables, PVEHs, setVariable, event handlers, nearestObjects, compile/preprocess diagnostics, render/simulation scope and performance timing.
systems.serverAuthority: Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md is the design/checklist layer for migrating client-trusted and payload-trusted gameplay flows to server-owned authority; patch order lives in Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md. Use ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md for DR-27 RequestSpecial/Nuke patch shape. DR-41/DR-44 confirm the same class has two network surfaces: registered PVF handlers and direct publicVariableServer channels such as ATTACK_WAVE_INIT and wfbe_supply_temp_*; OA PVEHs do not expose a trusted sender identity.
systems.icbmAuthority: ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md is the implementation-ready DR-27 guide. The client UI has local affordance gates, but the server currently trusts RequestSpecial "ICBM" payload side/team/objects before spawning NukeDammage. Server patching should re-derive commander/team, side, module/upgrade, funds/cost, impact anchor and idempotency before launch acceptance.
systems.attackWaveAuthority: Attack-Wave-Authority-Playbook.md is the DR-41 route. ATTACK_WAVE_INIT is a direct publicVariableServer channel outside PVF; server must re-derive side/supply from trusted state, add or use a server-verifiable requester anchor before validating authority, reject duplicates and preserve all-side-supply spend unless owner-approved design changes say otherwise.
systems.modules: Modules-Atlas.md is now the source-verified module map. It covers Nuke/ICBM (DR-27, CRITICAL client-authoritative superweapon), EASA, CM flares, IRS smoke, UAV (007 branch disabled), AntiStack, MASH, Skill classes, Valhalla low-gear, ZetaCargo sling, Engines stealth, AutoFlip, NEURO AI-taxi, CIPHER sort and Reaktiv. Use Deep-Review-Findings.md for DR details and Modules-Atlas.md for subsystem responsibilities/gates.
systems.victoryEndgame: Server/FSM/server_victory_threeway.sqf is the sole victory loop (80s cadence). DR-11 owns winner-inversion impact, DR-12 owns the threeway no-detection mode, DR-13 owns duplicate LogGameEnd context and DR-36 owns the root-cause mechanism plus clean Perf/JIP review. Server runtime, Feature Status and Hardening roadmap route there instead of repeating the full condition parsing.
systems.weatherDayNight: Server_DayNightCycle.sqf accelerated hybrid cycle; reviewed clean (Round 17, no defect) - no divide-by-zero, JIP-covered via engine-synced WFBE_DAYNIGHT_DATE + init setDate, server clock + per-machine local animation + 30s drift sync.
systems.markersCleanersRestorers: Map markers + Server/FSM cleaners (crater/mines/ruins/dropped-items) and restorers (buildings). DR-34: MASH map-marker feature dead both ends (trigger never broadcast + receiver commented Init_Client.sqf:132 + orphaned server PVEH). Cleaners/restorers not yet atlas'd (ledger Map partial). See AI-Headless-And-Performance.md.
systems.parametersLocalization: Mission parameters (Common/Init/Init_Parameters.sqf <- source Chernarus initJIPCompatible.sqf:132 / generated Vanilla :121; Rsc/Parameters.hpp; Dialogs.hpp:3136) and stringtable.xml localization. Reviewed clean (DR-35): no live missing localize/$STR_ keys; paramsArray index-aligned to class Params order. This is mission config class Params, not the unsafe SQF params command. DR-43a corrected 2026-06-04: local ignored/generated Chernarus and Vanilla version.sqf files exist, but Git does not track them; clean checkouts and generated/target roots still need version-file verification.
systems.economyAuthorityFirstCut: Economy-Authority-First-Cut.md is the source-backed minimum patch order for DR-22 side-supply clamp, DR-23 upgrades, DR-6 construction/defense and DR-14 player-buy ceiling.
systems.supplyMissionAuthorityCleanup: Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook.md is the source-backed patch guide for player supply missions and PR #1 supply helicopters: server-owned loaded/tracking state, DR-18 cooldown casing patch shape, cargo validation, Killed-handler idempotency, command-center scan narrowing and dedicated/JIP/heli validation. Supply-Mission-Architecture.md owns the flow/JIP map; Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-18 owns exact casing evidence.
systems.abandonedFeatureRevival: Abandoned-Feature-Revival-Review.md classifies dormant/broken feature paths: MASH markers need server-held/JIP-safe revival or removal; paratrooper markers are a small PVF registration revive; AI supply trucks are broken because compile is commented and supplytruck.fsm is missing; UAV 007/WASP actions/old upgrade dialog are stale cleanup; modded mission propagation needs an owner maintenance-model decision.
systems.performanceOpportunitySweep: Performance-Opportunity-Sweep.md ranks low-risk and high-value performance work: PVF dispatcher lookup, hosted FPS loop sleep, supply scan narrowing, public queue broadcast review, WASP marker polling and audit-first cleaner/marker-loop tuning. Current-state correction 2026-06-03: duplicate Skill_Init removal, hosted FPS loop sleep and narrowed supply command-center scan are docs/source propagated to maintained Vanilla but still need smoke and branch-specific release/master handling; factory queue counter/token cleanup remains current-code-change-needed; the WASP display-54 wait remains an open cleanup opportunity.

paratrooperMarkerRevival.summary: docs/source Chernarus and Vanilla Takistan register HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation and ship the sender/handler pair. Stable master and release a9219d88 omit the registration in both maintained missions; Arma smoke and modded mission drift cleanup remain.
sourceRefs: origin/release/2026-06-feature-bundle a9219d88 Chernarus Init_PublicVariables.sqf:41 NukeIncoming only; Vanilla :39 NukeIncoming only

systems.wikiMirrorReconciliation: Wiki-Mirror-Reconciliation-Plan.md remains the no-blind-copy policy. The active _wasp_wiki_tmp checkout is restored by scoped sync for current docs surfaces; alternate _wasp_wiki_claude still has broad divergence and must be reconciled file by file.

knownRisks: DR-43 (Low): external-research intake #2 (9 reports) corroborates the campaign (esp. report 8 = economy-authority thesis). Corrected 2026-06-04 source state: local ignored/generated Chernarus and Vanilla version.sqf files exist, but Git does not track them; clean checkouts and generated/target roots still need version-file verification before pack/test claims. Init_Server.sqf has duplicate compile/bind rows: LogGameEnd, PlayerObjectsList and AwardScorePlayer are live duplicate binds; InitAFKkickHandler, monitorServerFPS and MASH_MARKER are commented duplicate remnants. LogGameEnd dup ties DR-13.

DR-42 (Low/Med, headless): static-defence HC delegation never reports created units back to the server (update-back commented at Client_DelegateAIStaticDefence.sqf:28; town-AI reports via Client_DelegateTownAI.sqf:35). Server has no record of HC static-defence units, compounding DR-21. Backlog server-fps-hosted-loop-sleep duplicates DR-19.

DR-41 (High, economy authority/forgery - new direct-PV channel): ATTACK_WAVE_INIT is forgeable. Server_AttackWave.sqf takes _supply/_side directly from the client payload (no GetSideSupply re-derivation, no requester object or other trusted authority anchor, no server cost deduction); the 25000-supply gate is client-side only (updateclient.sqf:240). Forged _supply>=70000 (SUPPLY_MAX=50000) drives ATTACK_WAVE_PRICE_MODIFIER to 0 -> free units side-wide (larger -> negative pricing). Not in BattlEye publicvariable.txt. KEY: the forgery class has TWO surfaces - the PVF dispatcher (DR-1) and direct publicVariableServer channels (DR-41/DR-44) - so the server-authority redesign must re-derive trusted values in each direct PVEH too, not just validate the PVF dispatcher. OA PVEHs do not expose a trusted sender identity.

CONFIRMED 2026-06-02: Town AI vehicle despawn can delete a vehicle with a player passenger/crew member aboard if the player is not the group leader. server_town_ai.sqf:211-216 checks !(isPlayer leader group _x) and does not inspect crew/cargo/turret occupants. Use Town-AI-Vehicle-Despawn-Safety.md for the source chain, behavior-preserving guard shape and validation gates.

Direct attack-wave authority gap: ATTACK_WAVE_INIT is a direct publicVariable channel outside the generic PVF dispatcher. Common_AttackWaveActivate.sqf sends [_supply,_side], and Server_AttackWave.sqf uses client-supplied _supply for discount/timing. Server should re-derive supply from authoritative side state.

JIP robustness note: RequestJoin and AntiStack launch ACK have 30s resend loops but no terminal timeout; the later Init_Client.sqf post-join replicated-variable waits have no timeout/retry/log. Normal JIP is reviewed functional, but failures can hang silently.

Lifecycle source-check 2026-06-02: mission.sqm is part of town runtime bootstrap, not just map data. SQF-only scans miss town object Init_Town.sqf calls and WF_Logic Init_TownMode.sqf startup at mission.sqm:3265.

DR-40 (Low, WASP Perf nit + reviewed clean): WASP overlay Perf mostly clean; one busy-spin - global_marking_monitor.sqf:62 while{time<_this}do{findDisplay 54} polls every frame for up to 2s (input-disabled, one-time at init); should use the throttled waitUntil{sleep ...;cond} idiom like its :80 sibling. JIP/HC clean (per-client init from Init_Client; local-player guards; HC skips player-local). Old WASP/Init_Client.sqf init path is dead/commented (initJIPCompatible.sqf:243-244). MILESTONE: this was the last outstanding Perf/JIP-HC cell - every subsystem's Perf and JIP/HC dimension is now source-reviewed; residual Ã°Å¸Å¸Â¡ is exclusively Auth/PV owner decisions.

DR-39 (Low/Medium, supply missions Perf + abandoned-code; JIP clean): supplyMissionActive.sqf is a dead twin (compiled to WFBE_SE_FNC_SupplyMissionActive at Init_Server:81 but never called; superseded by the live supplyMissionStarted.sqf PVEH). The live per-mission server loop polls nearestObjects[pos,[],80] (all object types) every 3s to find a UAV terminal - narrow the type filter. JIP/HC is done correctly (the positive counterexample to DR-34): cooldown status is pull-based request/response so joiners query current state; the server-side tracking loop is truck-keyed and survives the starting player's disconnect. Minor: cooldown response is broadcast to all clients rather than targeted.

DR-38 (Low/Medium Perf, JIP clean): the PVF dispatchers Call Compile the sender-provided command string on every inbound message (Server/Client_HandlePVF.sqf), an avoidable per-message runtime recompile - redundant because PVFunctions are already pre-compiled at init into SRVFNC/CLTFNC globals (Init_PublicVariables.sqf:44/49). A validated getVariable lookup removes the recompile AND closes the DR-1 RCE in one change. JIP/HC dispatch is clean (registered at init on all machines incl JIP; PVFs are transient events; routing matches joiners).

DR-37 (Low, reviewed clean + robustness note): boot/lifecycle Perf clean (frame-throttled cheap waitUntils; the while{true}{sleep 0.1} loops at Init_Client.sqf:419/444 are bounded join-handshake polls with 30s retry, not perpetual). JIP/HC comprehensive+correct (initJIPCompatible role routing; time/date/teams/client-state synced via broadcast logic vars; RequestJoin handshake robust). Gap: the post-join serial waitUntil chain (Init_Client.sqf:367-502, wfbe_structures/commander/radio_hq/startpos/hq/hq_deployed/votetime) has NO timeouts -> a never-set synced variable hangs the JIP client forever with no fallback or log. Defensive-timeout improvement, not a live bug.

DR-36 (Medium correctness + Perf/JIP clean): victory loop (server_victory_threeway.sqf) is the source of DR-11/DR-13. Win check (:23) !(alive _hq)&&_factories==0 || _towns==_total&&!WFBE_GameOver parses (&&>||) so the !WFBE_GameOver guard covers only the towns clause, not the HQ-elimination clause; the side forEach (:43) has no break after a winner is set -> two same-tick eliminations double-fire endgame+LogGameEnd and overwrite WF_Winner with the opposite side (inversion). Perf clean (80s cadence); JIP server-authoritative (narrow endgame-join gap). Fix: parenthesize+guard both clauses + exitWith the forEach/while on gameOver.

DR-35 (Info, reviewed clean): parameters/localization reviewed - localization integrity clean (no live missing localize/$STR_ keys; 3 apparent misses = 1 casing FP, 1 engine STR_EP1_*, 2 dead/commented WASP actions). Mission parameter system live and correctly wired (Init_Parameters.sqf MP paramsArray / SP default; source Chernarus initJIPCompatible.sqf:132 / generated Vanilla :121; Dialogs.hpp:3136); this is class Params/paramsArray, not SQF params syntax. Fragility (not a defect): paramsArray is index-aligned to class Params order. Abandoned-code: WASP OnArmor + GearYourUnit actions are commented out in AddActions.sqf.

DR-34 (Low/Medium, broken/abandoned feature): the MASH map-marker feature is dead on both ends - client receiver commented out (Init_Client.sqf:132), the trigger PV WFBE_CL_MASH_MARKER_CREATED is never broadcast by any client, and the live server PVEH (Init_Server.sqf:70) is orphaned (listens for a never-sent PV). Deployed MASH tents (officer skill) therefore show no map markers. If revived, the publicVariable delivery is a single overwritten global: OA JIP can receive at most the last value, not the full marker set. Respawn selector is a harmless ~33Hz local marker-animation loop. Owner: revive (server-held list + JIP re-send/pull + unique names) or remove the dead code.

DR-33 (Medium, factory Perf+JIP/HC): unit production runs on the buyer's client. Current source still uses random varQueu token identity and the crewless-vehicle branch still exits before the normal queue decrement; source patch, Vanilla propagation, Arma smoke, buyer-disconnect behavior and public queu broadcast reduction remain.

DR-32 (Medium, generated-mission drift + abandoned code): vanilla Takistan is a faithful regeneration of the Chernarus source (logic byte-identical; only map-config differs) so DR-1..DR-31 apply verbatim. BUT the 7 modded missions are out of scope: Napf/eden/lingor are divergent hand-edited forks (100+ logic files differ incl security-critical PVF/victory/upgrade/HQ; not regenerated per DR-4 -> source fixes do NOT reach them; vuln classes persist with different lines), and sahrani/dingor/tavi/isladuala are abandoned stubs (1-20 files, non-runnable). Owner: complete-or-delete stubs; pick fork maintenance model; propagate fixes deliberately.

DR-31 (High, insecure deserialization): DiscordBot GameData.LoadFromFile() reads database.json with Newtonsoft TypeNameHandling.All every 60s (GameStatusUpdater timer) + at startup + on a command. $type gadget sink in the process that holds the Discord token. Not remotely exploitable as-configured (file written by the trusted local extension) but any write-primitive to the data dir = RCE. Trivial fix: TypeNameHandling.All -> None (data is a flat string[] DTO). Closes DR-29 #2 end-to-end. Discord secret hygiene is otherwise good (token.txt/preferences.json gitignored).

DR-30 (High, campaign-wide BE gap): the 'rely on BattlEye' remediation option cited in 8 prior findings (DR-1/6/14/16/22/23/27/28) is NOT shipped. Repo's entire BE footprint = 22-byte publicvariable.txt (single kickAFK feature rule, no default-deny, no forgery-PV restriction) + a README.docx; scripts.txt and all other BE filters absent. So nothing in-repo blunts PV forgery or the DR-1 call compile RCE; realistic remediation collapses to server-side authority in SQF. Caveat: BE filters normally live in the server BEpath outside the mission PBO -> confirm production posture with the server owner.

DR-29 (Medium, latent Critical, extension trust boundary): in-repo GLOBALGAMESTATS DLL is a one-way output-discarded telemetry exporter (no RCE-into-SQF, unlike AntiStack DB DR-7). Dormant deserialization-RCE landmine: commented load path uses Newtonsoft TypeNameHandling.Auto + DeserializeObject -> $type gadget RCE if re-enabled (and a load path is required for the feature to actually persist). Plus async-void create unawaited before File.Replace (first-run FileNotFound race; async-void exceptions can crash host) and a write-only/abandoned-refactor stub. Owner asks: delete/harden dead deser path, fix the I/O race.

DR-28 (High, economy authority): gear/EASA loadouts and vehicle rearm/repair/refuel/heal are client-authoritative (no server PVF; ChangePlayerFunds + client-side affordability tests only). Service rearm/refuel skip even the client guard. Completes the class: EVERY WFBE spend path (build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade/ICBM/gear) is client-authoritative -> single owner decision (server ledger vs BattlEye).

DR-27 (CRITICAL, network authority): ICBM nuke is forgeable. Any client can publicVariable RequestSpecial=["ICBM",side,baseObj,liveObj,team]; server RequestSpecial.sqf -> Server_HandleSpecial.sqf ICBM case spawns NukeDammage at the client-chosen position with zero authority validation (no per-side ICBM upgrade check, no commander check, no server funds check; menu gate + ChangePlayerFunds are client-only). Result: server-applied map-wide mass-kill from one forged PV. Same root cause/fix as economy class (DR-6/14/16/22/23) but match-wide blast radius -> higher priority.

Autonomous AI supply trucks are config-gated latent breakage: UpdateSupplyTruck compile is disabled, a live call remains under Supply System 0 plus AI commanders, and Server/FSM/supplytruck.fsm is missing.
DoNotUse CRV7PG loadout classes can crash the game if used in loadouts.
AntiStack database extension calls are live-server sensitive.
Generated missions can be overwritten by LoadoutManager.
PublicVariable traffic and client marker/UI loops are performance-sensitive.
PVF dispatch uses Call Compile on registered function-name strings; keep command names controlled.
PR #1 currently stacks Killed event handlers on reused supply vehicles; current double-payment is bounded, but the event-handler leak should be fixed before adding side effects.
Client UI has duplicate dialog IDD 23000 for EASA and Economy; avoid findDisplay assumptions and use unique IDDs for new dialogs.
OptionsAvailable and RscOverlay share title IDD 10200; use uiNamespace display handles rather than IDD uniqueness.
UI/marker loops are performance-sensitive polling loops; preserve cached writes, map-closed backoff and mission-parameter gates.

DR-1 (High, hardening): Server_HandlePVF.sqf/Client_HandlePVF.sqf Call Compile the sender-chosen command string with no validation, and BattlEyeFilter/publicvariable.txt has only the kickAFK feature rule. Validate the command set before compile and add a real BE PV filter before trusting on a public server.

DR-1 fix (behavior-preserving): replace 'Spawn (Call Compile _script)' with 'Spawn (missionNamespace getVariable [_script, {}])' in both HandlePVF dispatchers; SRVFNC/CLTFNC are globals so legitimate traffic is unchanged and hostile strings no-op. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 3 playbook.

Residual after DR-1 fix: the PVF model still trusts client-sent command names and payload values, for example RequestChangeScore sets score from payload. Arbitrary code execution is closed, but legitimate-command forgery needs separate per-handler sender/payload validation.
DR-2/DR-3: paratrooper drop markers and MASH tent markers are confirmed dead receiver-side paths.

DR-4: Chernarus and Takistan differ only by LoadoutManager skip-list/blacklist, but skip-listed files never propagate and must be hand-mirrored; Modded_Missions/* are abandoned until modded propagation is re-enabled.

Construction hardening gap: coin_interface.sqf performs cost/placement/role checks client-side, then RequestStructure/RequestDefense do only light server class-existence validation before creating server objects.

DR-6 (High, gameplay integrity): RequestStructure/RequestDefense take _side (and _manned) from the client payload and RequestMHQRepair passes the raw payload to MHQRepair; no commander/funds/dead-HQ/base-area checks server-side. A modified client can forge free factories, AI-manned defenses/minefields and HQ repairs for any side. Root cause: payloads omit the player and Arma2 PVEH has no sender identity. Fix: add player to payload + validate side+commander+funds server-side. Pairs with DR-1 (DR-1 stops code execution; DR-6 stops forged legit commands). See Deep-Review-Findings Round 4.

DR-7 (High, external trust): all 7 Server/Module/AntiStack/callDatabase*.sqf 'call compile' the A2WaspDatabase callExtension return Ã¢â‚¬â€ server executes the external DLL stdout as SQF. DLL is not in the repo; ANTISTACK defaults ON (Init_CommonConstants.sqf:171). Empty/garbage return -> call compile '' -> nil select 0 throws. A2 OA has no parseSimpleArray; fix = guard empty + shape-check (ARRAY of SCALARs) before reading. DR-8 blocking join/skill poll (<=12s/<=27s); DR-9 SEND_PLAYERLIST vs callExtension length limits; DR-10 defaults-on against an absent DLL. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 5.

Player buy-unit authority gap: GUI_Menu_BuyUnits.sqf deducts funds and spawns Client_BuildUnit locally; there is no registered RequestBuyUnit PVF and no Server/PVFunctions/RequestBuyUnit.sqf in the repo.
Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit latent path: AIBuyUnit is compiled in Server/Init/Init_Server.sqf, but the current repo-wide static scan found no caller outside Server_BuyUnit.sqf itself.
AI commander automation partial: constants/state/funds exist, but no active server loop/FSM was found to set wfbe_aicom_running = true.

Server_AssignNewCommander DR-15 current status: current source/Vanilla still use _side = _this in Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf and RequestNewCommander still sends the duplicate caller-side notification; source patch, Vanilla propagation, requester/role validation and Arma smoke remain.
Supply mission cooldown casing mismatch: town init seeds lastSupplyMissionRun while server supply mission code reads/writes LastSupplyMissionRun.

Hosted server FPS loop current status: docs/source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla now early-exit serverFpsGUI.sqf and monitorServerFPS.sqf on !isDedicated before the loops. Stable origin/master still has the old inner-isDedicated sleep shape; release a9219d88 carries Chernarus-only coverage while release Vanilla remains old. Arma smoke remains.

LoadoutManager root discovery current status: the tool now accepts either an ancestor named a2waspwarfare or repo-root markers (Missions, Missions_Vanilla and Tools/LoadoutManager/LoadoutManager.csproj), so work/a style checkouts are runnable.
LoadoutManager packaging current status: dotnet run still calls ZipManager after generation/copy, but A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP=1 skips _MISSIONS.7z creation so propagation-only runs do not require 7za.
BattlEyeFilter/publicvariable.txt is feature-specific for kickAFK and is not a comprehensive PV hardening layer.

DR-11 (Med-High, correctness): server_victory_threeway.sqf merges a lose-test (HQ dead + no factories) and a win-test (holds all towns) into one if and calls WFBE_CO_FNC_LogGameEnd (arg=winner) with the OPPOSITE of _x, so the persisted *_WIN_CHERNARUS profile tally is inverted for all-towns victories. WF_Winner is a dead write. && before || + !WFBE_GameOver guards only the towns branch + no forEach break -> endgame can double-fire. DR-12 threeway victory mode has no detection (WFBE_C_VICTORY_THREEWAY default 0; detection gated if(_victory==0); sole gameOver/failMission setter). DR-13 duplicate buggy Server/PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 6.

DR-14 (High, architectural): player unit purchasing has NO server authority Ã¢â‚¬â€ GUI_Menu_BuyUnits.sqf spawns BuildUnit (client createVehicle/createUnit) and deducts client-side; no RequestBuyUnit PVF. With wfbe_funds broadcast-writable, the player economy is fully client-trusted. This is the ceiling on DR-1/DR-6 hardening; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter (not a PV filter) can constrain client createVehicle. DR-15 current status: Server_AssignNewCommander call-shape and duplicate notification remain patch-ready/current-source-unpatched; RequestNewCommander still needs requester/role validation and Arma smoke. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 7 and Commander-Reassignment-Call-Shape.md.

DR-16 (High): structure sale (GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf:104-152) is fully client-authoritative Ã¢â‚¬â€ client commander check, client refund, client setDammage 1 destruction; no server PVF. With DR-6 (build) and DR-14 (buy), the entire WFBE economy is client-authoritative; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter or a server-PVF redesign can enforce it. DR-17 (confirms Curie): RscMenu_EASA and RscMenu_Economy share idd=23000 (Rsc/Dialogs.hpp:3211,3289) -> findDisplay 23000 ambiguous. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 8.

DR-18 (Med, confirms Cicero): supply-cooldown key casing mismatch Ã¢â‚¬â€ Init_Town.sqf:35 seeds 'lastSupplyMissionRun' (lowercase) but supply logic uses 'LastSupplyMissionRun' (capital); setVariable keys are case-sensitive -> 0-seed dead, key nil first check -> isSupplyMissionActiveInTown.sqf:11 nil+interval throws. Fix: align casing or getVariable default 0. DR-19 (Med, non-dedicated, confirms Cicero): old serverFpsGUI.sqf + monitorServerFPS.sqf put sleep 8 inside if(isDedicated) -> hosted/listen busy-loop. Current docs/source has the early-exit fix in Chernarus/Vanilla; stable master remains old; release a9219d88 is Chernarus-only for this lane. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 9 and Current-Source-Status-Snapshot.

DR-20 (High, multiplayer correctness): the HQ Killed EH is registered on every owning-side client (set-hq-killed-eh broadcast + Init_Client.sqf:500-503 JIP path), but Server_OnHQKilled.sqf has no idempotency guard -> on mobile-HQ death the server runs it once per owning-side client (~2N x killer score award + N messages; score exploit on populated servers). JIP detection itself is correct. Fix: per-HQ processed flag in OnHQKilled (detect redundantly, act once). See Deep-Review-Findings Round 10.

DR-21 (Med, perf/operational): HC delegation has no failover. On HC disconnect Arma2 OA migrates its local AI to the server (load spike, NOT orphaned/lost Ã¢â‚¬â€ corrects round-1 hypothesis); Server_OnPlayerDisconnected.sqf does not redirect future delegation and WFBE_C_AI_DELEGATION is only evaluated at boot, so a reconnecting HC doesn't resume offload. Corrected 2026-06-02: setGroupOwner/groupOwner are Arma 3 1.40, not OA 1.64; Wasp can redirect future spawns but cannot live-transfer already-running groups in OA SQF. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 11 and Arma-2-OA-Command-Version-Reference.

DR-22 (High, economy exploit): Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf clamp 'if (_change<0) then {_change=_currentSupply-_amount}' is a broken floor Ã¢â‚¬â€ _amount is signed, so overspending yields currentSupply+|amount| (spend 300 from 100 -> 400, a windfall). Live in both west/east handlers; identical block in Common_ChangeSideSupply is dead. Fix: _change=0. Confirms Faraday candidate + round-1 note. No resistance-side handler. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 12.

DR-23 (High, economy integrity): RequestUpgrade.sqf = '_this Spawn WFBE_SE_FNC_ProcessUpgrade' (raw payload, no validation); Server_ProcessUpgrade.sqf does no commander/funds/sequence/dependency check and never deducts cost (client-side). Forge free upgrades; client-controlled indices -> out-of-range error. SYNTHESIS: with DR-6/14/16/22/23 the entire WFBE economy (build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade) is client-authoritative Ã¢â‚¬â€ one architectural decision (server-PVF validation vs BattlEye scripts.txt) covers the class. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 13.

DR-24 (Low, dead code): RscMenu_Upgrade (Rsc/Dialogs.hpp:2425) onLoad ExecVMs Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf which does not exist (only GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf); dialog never opened; live upgrade UI is GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf. Dead/naming-drift reference. Delete or repoint. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 14.

DR-25a (Low): RscOverlay (Titles.hpp:46) + OptionsAvailable (Titles.hpp:165) share idd=10200. DR-25b (Low): RscClickableText.soundPush[]={, 0.2, 1} (Ressources.hpp:556) malformed (empty leading element; correct {"", 0.2, 1}); base control class. Both confirm Curie candidates; assign distinct IDDs and fix the array. See Deep-Review-Findings Round 15.

DR-26 (Low, governance): LICENSE.md is a custom/proprietary license (Copyright Spayker 2016 / Miksuu 2025; contributions assigned to the repo owner; reuse/distribution restricted), NOT OSI -> source-available, not open-source. Resolves external deep-research reports' 'license unspecified'. Also: DiscordBot/preferences_sample.json ships a real GuildID + hardcoded DataSourcePath (neutralize); no CI (only FUNDING.yml). See Deep-Review-Findings Round 16.

Gear/EASA risk: buy-gear and EASA flows are mostly client-authoritative for purchase/deduction/equip decisions; treat them as gameplay-integrity risks until server validation or BattlEye script filtering is designed.
Gear profile risk: Client_UI_Gear_SaveTemplateProfile.sqf references _u_upgrade in template filtering without a local definition; verify before extending template/profile behavior.

External deep-research note: the 3 PDFs Steff supplied were generated FROM this wiki (raw.githubusercontent wiki citations) + Miksuu upstream proxy -> downstream corroboration of DR-1/DR-6/DR-7 etc., not independent source verification. Claude's source-verified DR findings are a superset (reports lack DR-11/15/18/19/20/22/23).

Direct PV hardening gap: attack waves, side supply, supply missions, MASH markers, HQ state, AntiStack compensation, FPS, day/night, marker/message and AFK channels bypass the generic WFBE_PVF command lists; a PVF dispatcher fix alone does not harden them.

Integration deployment gap: building the in-repo Extension project only provides a2waspwarfare_Extension/GLOBALGAMESTATS; AntiStack depends on a separate A2WaspDatabase DLL that is absent from the repo.
Governance/tooling gap: DiscordBot sample config has real-looking IDs and production-style path defaults, and the repo has no CI for .NET builds, SQF reference checks or generated-mission drift.

currentPR.title: feat: supply helicopters for both teams
currentPR.deferred: Autonomous AI-flown supply helicopters until AI supply logistics is restored/redesigned.
reviewRisks: Repeated supply vehicle reloads must not stack duplicate Killed event handlers.
PR #1 behavior is pending until merged.

findings: PVF dispatch uses one PV variable per command with client-side element-0 routing and scheduled Spawn dispatch.
Town AI performance is based on spawn/delete distance activation, not simulation caching.
HC delegation works by remote creation and can downgrade at init if no HC is present.
Supply System 0 plus AI commanders can hit disabled UpdateSupplyTruck and missing supplytruck.fsm.
PR #1 stacks Killed event handlers on reused supply vehicles.
correctionsDuringIntegration: Lifecycle economy override is WF_Debug-gated in current source, not unconditional.

findings: DR-1 PV dispatch Call Compile trust boundary unmitigated by validation or BattlEye filter.

DR-2 paratrooper drop markers remain patch-ready/current-source-unpatched: current source/Vanilla have the sender and handler file, but lack HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation in the client PVF list; source patch, Vanilla propagation and Arma smoke remain.
DR-3 MASH tent markers dead because receiverMASHmarker.sqf is only referenced by a commented client init compile.
DR-4 Chernarus and Takistan are in sync except skip-list/blacklist differences; Modded_Missions are stale or stubbed.
DR-5 atlas preprocessFile counts should be treated as point-in-time; FSM count of 3 verified accurate.
DR-1 fix is behavior-preserving: SRVFNC/CLTFNC are missionNamespace globals, so dispatch can resolve via getVariable [_script, {}] instead of Call Compile; hostile strings become no-ops.
No registered PVF handler compiles its parameters; no second-order injection on the PVF path.
Residual: legitimate-command forgery, such as RequestChangeScore trusting payload, is not closed by the dispatch fix and needs separate per-handler sender/payload validation.

DR-6: RequestStructure/RequestDefense take _side (+_manned) from the client payload; RequestMHQRepair passes the raw payload to MHQRepair. Only class-existence is checked server-side; no commander/funds/dead-HQ/base-area validation.
Forgeable to mint free factories, AI-manned defenses/minefields and HQ repairs for any side, bypassing economy and base-area avail (decremented only client-side).

Root cause: payloads omit the requesting player and Arma2 PVEH has no sender identity; authority must be reconstructed server-side. Playbook: add player to payload + validate side+commander+funds. Pairs with DR-1.
DR-7 (High): all 7 callDatabase*.sqf call compile the A2WaspDatabase callExtension return Ã¢â‚¬â€ server executes external DLL stdout as SQF; DLL absent from repo; defaults ON.
DR-8 blocking DB poll on join/skill path; DR-9 SEND_PLAYERLIST vs callExtension length limits; DR-10 defaults-on against an absent DLL.
A2 OA has no parseSimpleArray; fix = defensive shape-validation (guard empty + assert ARRAY of SCALARs) before reading, plus circuit-breaker, chunking and DLL auto-detect.

reviewPasses.type: read-only sub-agent source report integrated by Codex
findings: Direct publicVariable channels include day/night, messages, markers, ICBM/radiation, side supply, supply missions, anti-stack, AFK and server FPS.
Replacing PVF dispatcher Call Compile does not close legitimate-command forgery; high-trust handlers still need sender/role/funds/object validation.
BattlEye publicvariable.txt contains only the kickAFK feature rule.

reviewPasses.type: read-only sub-agent source report integrated by Codex
findings: RscMenu_Upgrade points to missing GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf while live flow uses WFBE_UpgradeMenu.
RscClickableText.soundPush[] has a suspect malformed config value.
Buy gear has documented partial work around target refresh, vehicle target content and template scope.

reviewPasses.type: read-only sub-agent source report integrated by Codex
findings: LoadoutManager supports work/a style checkouts through repo-root marker discovery; an ancestor literally named a2waspwarfare is no longer required.
LoadoutManager packaging can be skipped for propagation-only runs with A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP=1; release packaging still needs 7za.
Mission game stats flow through a2waspwarfare_Extension to C:\a2waspwarfare\Data\database.json, then DiscordBot polls that file every 60 seconds.
Modded mission generation is disabled/commented; Modded_Missions/* are stale/non-authoritative.

reviewPasses.type: read-only sub-agent source report integrated by Codex
findings: Server runtime starts town, camp, town AI, resources, victory, cleanup, FPS and AntiStack loops from Init_Server.sqf.
AI commander state/funds/constants exist, but no active AI commander loop/FSM was found.

Server_AssignNewCommander DR-15 call-shape bug remains patch-ready/current-source-unpatched; remaining work is source patching, Vanilla propagation, RequestNewCommander authority validation and Arma smoke.
Supply mission reward/cooldown logic has client-authority and variable-casing risks.
Hosted/non-dedicated server FPS publishers remain patch-ready/current-source-unpatched; source patch, Vanilla propagation and Arma smoke remain.
Player buy-unit path is client-local and does not use a RequestBuyUnit PVF.
RequestBuyUnit is not registered in Common/Init/Init_PublicVariables.sqf and no Server/PVFunctions/RequestBuyUnit.sqf exists.
Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit is compiled but appears latent unless a dynamic caller is later proven.
Factory spawn pads and queue state are convention-heavy and should be preserved carefully.
Spawned four read-only Codex sub-agents for independent deep-index lanes: network/PV, server gameplay loops, UI/HUD/dialogs and tooling/integrations.
Progress is tracked in Progress-Dashboard.md, Coordination-Board.md, agent-status.json, agent-collaboration.json and agent-events.jsonl.

DR-11 (Med-High): victory winner reported inconsistently; persisted *_WIN_CHERNARUS tally inverted for all-towns victories (LogGameEnd called with opposite of _x); WF_Winner is a dead write; precedence/guard gap allows double-fire.
DR-12: threeway victory mode (WFBE_C_VICTORY_THREEWAY != 0) has no detection in the only victory script -> matches never auto-end.
DR-13: duplicate buggy Server/PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf (getVariable result used as setVariable key; bare-global WEST_WIN_CHERNARUS).

reviewPasses.type: authority review + adversarial cross-check of Codex/Cicero
findings: DR-14 (High, architectural): player purchasing has no server authority (client createVehicle/createUnit + client-side deduct; no RequestBuyUnit PVF). Ceiling on DR-1/DR-6 hardening; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter can constrain it.

DR-15 (current status corrected 2026-06-02T17:45): Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf still uses '_side = _this' and current source/Vanilla still duplicate new-commander-assigned from the caller/helper path. RequestNewCommander authority validation remains after the correctness patch.
Verified Codex factory-purchase-atlas (no RequestBuyUnit) and Cicero's commander-assign candidate at source.

reviewPasses.type: authority review + adversarial cross-check of Curie
findings: DR-16 (High): structure sale (GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf:104-152) is fully client-authoritative (client commander check, client refund, client setDammage 1); completes build(DR-6)/buy(DR-14)/sell(DR-16) all client-authoritative.
DR-17 (confirms Curie): RscMenu_EASA and RscMenu_Economy both idd=23000 (Rsc/Dialogs.hpp:3211,3289) -> findDisplay 23000 ambiguous.
Remaining UI follow-ups: shared title IDD 10200, stale RscMenu_Upgrade, RscClickableText.soundPush.
DR-18 confirmed: supply-cooldown key casing mismatch (lowercase seed vs capital readers; case-sensitive keys) -> nil-throw on first check; defeats the 0-seed guard.

DR-19 current status: docs/source Chernarus and maintained Vanilla have the early !isDedicated exit before both FPS publisher loops; stable origin/master still has the old inner-isDedicated sleep; release a9219d88 is Chernarus-only for this lane; two redundant FPS publishers remain a separate compatibility/consolidation decision.
Both were Cicero candidates from Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas; verified at source with exact impact and one-line fixes.

DR-20 (High): HQ Killed EH registered on every owning-side client (broadcast + JIP init), but Server_OnHQKilled.sqf has no idempotency guard -> N-fold duplicate processing (score exploit) on mobile-HQ death.
JIP HQ-death detection itself is correct (Init_Client !_isDeployed guard; deployed HQ has server-side EH).
Fix: per-HQ processed flag in OnHQKilled; keep redundant EH registration (detect redundantly, act once).
Self-correction: round-1 'HC disconnect orphans units' is wrong Ã¢â‚¬â€ engine migrates local units to server (no loss).
DR-21 (Med): HC delegation has no failover/rebalancing; disconnect dumps AI on the server and delegation is only evaluated at boot.
OA has no setGroupOwner/groupOwner live-transfer path; future HC recovery can only redirect new AI spawns and improve reconnect routing.
DR-22 (High): Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf broken floor turns overspend into a windfall (currentSupply+|amount|). Fix: _change=0.
Same block in Common_ChangeSideSupply is dead (server recomputes from _amount).
Confirms Faraday's negative-delta candidate + round-1 note; resistance-side handler present.
DR-23 (High): upgrade purchasing client-authoritative Ã¢â‚¬â€ RequestUpgrade passes raw payload to ProcessUpgrade; no commander/funds/sequence/dependency check, no server-side cost.
Synthesis: entire WFBE economy (build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade = DR-6/14/16/22/23) is client-authoritative; one architectural decision (server-PVF vs BattlEye) covers the class.
Confirms Faraday's upgrade-authority candidate.
DR-24 (Low): RscMenu_Upgrade onLoad ExecVMs missing GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf; dialog never opened; live UI is GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf. Dead/naming-drift.
Handed a reliable missing-reference scanner to Codex/tooling (my ad-hoc bash scan mishandled Windows backslash paths).
Severity gradient: campaign now in the long tail (Low/cleanup), indicating high-traffic cells are largely reviewed.
DR-25a (Low): RscOverlay + OptionsAvailable share title idd=10200.
DR-25b (Low): RscClickableText.soundPush[] malformed ({, 0.2, 1}) at Ressources.hpp:556.
Both confirm Curie's remaining UI candidates; UI follow-ups closed.
3 deep-research PDFs are downstream of our wiki (corroboration, not independent source verification); they re-derive DR-1/6/7 etc.; our DR findings are a superset.
DR-26 (Low, governance): LICENSE.md is custom/proprietary (not OSI); resolves reports' 'license unspecified'.
Governance handoffs to Codex/owner: neutralize DiscordBot sample GuildID/DataSourcePath; add CI (SQF syntax + generated-mission drift + .NET build).

Weather/day-night reviewed clean: no div-by-zero (twilight weight constant=3; day duration min 1), JIP covered (engine-synced WFBE_DAYNIGHT_DATE + init setDate), local-animation + 30s drift-sync design sound.
Recorded the negative result so future passes don't re-review; ledger weather cell -> reviewed-clean.
Integrity: not manufacturing a finding where code is correct is as valuable as finding bugs.
Gear catalog arrays, buy-gear templates and EASA aircraft loadouts are split across mission config, client UI and generated LoadoutManager output.
EASA and balance output are generated by Tools/LoadoutManager and should be edited at the tool data/source layer where possible.
Gear/EASA purchase and equip decisions remain client-authoritative and should be treated as gameplay-integrity risks.

pagesAdded: Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md (Modules row -> Map done, Auth/PV/Perf/JIP-HC partial)
reviewPasses.findings: DR-27 (CRITICAL): ICBM nuke fully client-authoritative. nukeincoming.sqf:23 sends RequestSpecial=[ICBM,...]; RequestSpecial.sqf=_this Spawn HandleSpecial; Server_HandleSpecial.sqf:97-112 spawns NukeDammage at client-chosen position with no upgrade/commander/funds check. waitUntil !alive _target is timing-only. One forged PV = server-applied map-wide kill. Apex of client-authoritative class. Also confirmed UAV-007 branch disabled; rest of Client/Module config-gated cosmetic/QoL.

pagesAdded: Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md (Gear/EASA row Auth->done; Economy note extended)
reviewPasses.findings: DR-28 (High): gear/EASA loadouts + vehicle rearm/repair/refuel/heal are client-authoritative. No server PVF; EASA cost is client-side if(_funds>price)+ChangePlayerFunds (GUI_Menu_EASA.sqf:46-50); Service rearm (:196-200) and refuel (:217-219) deduct unconditionally (no affordability guard, unlike repair/heal). Completes economy-authority class (build/buy/sell/supply/upgrade/ICBM/gear all client-authoritative).

DR-29 (Medium, latent Critical): in-repo GLOBALGAMESTATS callExtension DLL. Safe today (output never written/discarded -> no call compile, contrast DR-7; reflection enum-gated). Risks: dormant deser-RCE landmine (commented TypeNameHandling.Auto+DeserializeObject); write-only/abandoned-refactor stub (no cross-restart persistence; load path commented, references Database not GameData); async-void create unawaited before File.Replace -> first-run race + possible host crash; minor SQF abs(playerCount-1) HC misreport.

DR-30 (High, campaign-wide): repo BattlEye footprint is a 22-byte publicvariable.txt with one rule (5 "kickAFK" = AFK-kick feature plumbing, not security). No default-deny -> no restriction on forgery-class PVs (RequestSpecial/RequestStructure/RequestUpgrade/HandlePVF). scripts.txt ABSENT -> nothing blunts DR-1 call compile RCE. Implication: option (b) rely-on-BattlEye across DR-1/6/14/16/22/23/27/28 is not shipped; remediation collapses to (a) server-side authority. Caveat: BE files normally live in server BEpath outside the PBO -> production posture is an owner question.

pagesAdded: Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md (Integrations row: all 4 sub-targets done, Map complete)
reviewPasses.findings: DR-31 (High): DiscordBot GameData.LoadFromFile() deserializes database.json with TypeNameHandling.All (Newtonsoft gadget sink, worse than the dormant .Auto in DR-29), run every 60s by GameStatusUpdater timer + startup + a command. Capability gratuitous (flat string[] DTO; writer uses .None). Not remote as-configured but any write to the data path = RCE in the token-holding bot process. Fix: .All->.None + delete dead .Auto. Secondary: secret hygiene GOOD (token.txt+preferences.json gitignored; sample tokenless); commands auth-gated; 3-way exportedArgs shape drift (ext[2]/bot[4]/SQF 5).

reviewPasses.type: PV/direct-channel and external integration docs integration
findings: Direct non-PVF channels include attack waves, side supply, supply missions, MASH markers, HQ state, AntiStack compensation, server FPS, AFK, day/night and marker/message channels.
A WFBE_PVF dispatcher fix or whitelist does not cover those direct channels; BattlEye publicvariable design must include both registered and direct channels.
The in-repo a2waspwarfare_Extension/GLOBALGAMESTATS path is separate from the absent out-of-repo A2WaspDatabase AntiStack extension.
DiscordBot sample/config hygiene and missing CI/reference validation are documented governance/tooling follow-ups.
Claude DR-27 was linked from Networking and Feature Status as the highest-priority registered-command authority hazard.
Claude DR-28 was linked from Economy, Gear/EASA and Feature Status as the final confirmed spend path in the client-authoritative economy class.
Claude DR-29, DR-30 and DR-31 are now linked from External Integrations / Feature Status as integration and hosting hardening follow-ups.

reviewPasses.findings: DR-32 (Medium, drift + abandoned-code): 3 fidelity tiers. Vanilla Takistan faithful (15/671 sqf differ, all map-config + textures; logic byte-identical -> DR-1..DR-31 propagate verbatim). Napf/eden/lingor divergent hand-edited forks (104-123 logic files differ incl security-critical; Napf ICBM adds GBU-12 bombs; not regenerated per DR-4). sahrani/dingor/tavi/isladuala abandoned stubs (1-20 files, non-runnable).

DR-33 (Medium). Current source Client_BuildUnit.sqf still uses random varQueu token identity and the crewless-vehicle branch still exits before the normal queue decrement. Remaining DR-33 work is source patching, Vanilla propagation, Arma smoke, buyer-disconnect behavior review and public queu broadcast reduction.

DR-34 (Low/Medium, broken feature): MASH map-marker dead on both ends - client receiver commented (Init_Client.sqf:132), trigger PV WFBE_CL_MASH_MARKER_CREATED never broadcast, server PVEH live-but-orphaned (Init_Server.sqf:70). MASH tents real (Officer_Undeploy_MASH.sqf) but no markers. Confirms+extends DR-2. Latent JIP gap if revived: publicVariable is a single overwritten global, so OA JIP can receive at most the last value, not the full marker set. Perf: respawn selector ~33Hz sleep-0.03 local loop (bounded). Minor: round random 50000 non-unique marker name.

DR-35 (Info, reviewed clean): localization integrity clean - all 204 static localize keys resolve except 3 (1 casing FP, 1 engine STR_EP1_*, 2 in dead/commented WASP actions); config $STR_ all resolve; no live broken string. Mission parameter system live+wired (Init_Parameters.sqf paramsArray MP/default SP <- source Chernarus initJIPCompatible.sqf:132 / generated Vanilla :121; display via Dialogs.hpp:3136); paramsArray index-aligned to class Params order (fragility note). This is class Params/paramsArray, not SQF params syntax. Abandoned: WASP OnArmor + GearYourUnit actions commented in AddActions.sqf. Method: Arma stringtable lookup is case-insensitive -> case-fold before diffing + check liveness or you get false missing-key findings.

DR-36. Victory Perf clean (80s _loopTimer, cheap checks, dead _innerTimer). JIP/HC server-authoritative; narrow gap: endgame SendToClients + unbroadcast WFBE_GameOver not replayed to a player joining the ~10s pre-failMission window (moot). SOURCE MECHANISM for DR-11/DR-13: win check (:23) parses (&&>||) so !WFBE_GameOver guards only the towns clause not the HQ-elimination clause; side forEach (:43) has no break -> two same-tick eliminations double-fire endgame+LogGameEnd and overwrite WF_Winner with the opposite side. Re-confirms DR-12 (threeway _victory!=0 skips detection). Fix: guard both clauses + break on winner.

DR-37 (Low, reviewed clean + robustness note): Boot Perf clean - frame-throttled bare waitUntils with cheap conditions; Init_Client.sqf:248 throttle idiom waitUntil{sleep 0.5;cond}; while{true}{sleep 0.1...exitWith} at :419/:444 are bounded join-handshake polls (ACK exit, 30s retry), not perpetual. JIP/HC correct: initJIPCompatible routes server/client-II/HC; JIP syncs time/date+teams+all client state via broadcast logic vars; RequestJoin handshake has 30s retry + lobby fallback. Robustness gap: post-join serial waitUntil chain (Init_Client.sqf:367-502) has no timeouts -> a never-set synced var hangs JIP client forever with no fallback/log.

DR-38 (Low/Medium Perf + JIP clean): PVF dispatchers do Spawn (Call Compile _script) per inbound message (Server_HandlePVF.sqf:14, Client_HandlePVF.sqf:22) - avoidable per-message recompile, redundant vs the init-time precompile into SRVFNC/CLTFNC globals (Init_PublicVariables.sqf:44/49). Validated getVariable lookup removes the recompile AND closes DR-1 RCE (one fix). JIP/HC clean: dispatchers registered in Init_PublicVariables (:45/:50) via Init_Common on all machines incl JIP; PVFs are transient events (no replay); destination routing matches joiners.

DR-39 (Low/Medium). Abandoned-code: supplyMissionActive.sqf is a dead twin (plain body, no PVEH, compiled Init_Server:81 but never called), superseded by live supplyMissionStarted.sqf (self-registers PVEH). Perf current status: source/Vanilla still use [] for the 80-meter command-center scan before the Base_WarfareBUAVterminal guard, while the 8-meter nearby-player scan intentionally remains broad. JIP/HC done right (vs DR-34): cooldown status is pull-based request/response (WFBE_Client_PV_IsSupplyMissionActiveInTown -> server compute -> WFBE_Server_PV_IsSupplyMissionActiveInTown -> client store) so joiners query state; server-side loop survives starter disconnect (truck-keyed). Minor: cooldown answer broadcast to all not targeted.

DR-40 (Low). WASP Perf mostly clean; one nit: global_marking_monitor.sqf:62 while{time<_this}do{findDisplay 54} is a sleepless busy-spin (<=2s, input-disabled, one-time) - convert to throttled waitUntil like its :80 sibling. baserep 1Hz only while repairing; DropRPG sleep 30 cooldown; AddActions one-shot wait. JIP/HC clean: live WASP wired per-client from Init_Client (:15/:267/:574/:575) so joiners init locally; local-player guards correct; HC skips. Dead: old WASP/Init_Client.sqf path commented in initJIPCompatible.sqf:243-244. MILESTONE: last Perf/JIP-HC cell - all Perf+JIP/HC dimensions now reviewed; residual is Auth/PV owner decisions.

DR-41 (High, economy forgery; first collaboration-follow pass): confirmed Codex pv-scout candidate. ATTACK_WAVE_INIT is a forgeable DIRECT publicVariable - Server_AttackWave.sqf:5-6 trusts client _supply/_side wholesale (no GetSideSupply re-derivation, no sender check, no cost deduction); client gate (updateclient.sqf:240, GetSideSupply>=25000) is advisory only. Forged _supply>=70000 (SUPPLY_MAX=50000) -> ATTACK_WAVE_PRICE_MODIFIER 0 -> free units side-wide; larger -> negative pricing. Not in BattlEye filter. Extends the forgery class; ARCHITECTURE: class has TWO surfaces - PVF dispatcher (DR-1) AND direct publicVariableServer channels (DR-41) - server-authority redesign must cover both.

DR-42 (Low/Med, headless): static-defence HC delegation update-back commented out (Client_DelegateAIStaticDefence.sqf:28) vs town-AI reports back (Client_DelegateTownAI.sqf:35); server never tracks HC static-defence units -> compounds DR-21. DR-19 dedup: backlog server-fps-hosted-loop-sleep == DR-19.

DR-43: 9 new deep-research reports corroborate DR-1..DR-42 (report 8 = independent restatement of economy-authority thesis + DR-1; downstream syntheses, not source-verification). Corrected 2026-06-04: local ignored/generated Chernarus and Vanilla version.sqf files exist, but Git does not track them, so the remaining version-file issue is clean-checkout and generated/target-root verification. Init_Server.sqf duplicate bind cleanup remains: LogGameEnd/PlayerObjectsList/AwardScorePlayer live duplicates; InitAFKkickHandler/monitorServerFPS/MASH_MARKER commented remnants.

toolingProjects.name: Tools/LoadoutManager
requiredExternalFiles: 7za on PATH or configured for packaging
toolingProjects.notes: Requires either an ancestor named a2waspwarfare or repo-root markers such as Missions, Missions_Vanilla and Tools/LoadoutManager/LoadoutManager.csproj; work/a style checkouts are supported. Set A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP=1 for propagation-only runs that should skip _MISSIONS.7z packaging.

toolingProjects.name: Tools/PerformanceAuditAnalyzer
toolingProjects.notes: Read-only log analyzer useful after performance-sensitive mission changes.

toolingProjects.name: DiscordBot
toolingProjects.notes: Runtime refuses missing/empty token.txt. Do not invent secrets.

toolingProjects.name: Extension
toolingProjects.notes: Exports _RVExtension@12 and serializes GameData for DiscordBot.

toolingProjects.name: Mods/mkswf_sidewinder_reload_time_fix
toolingProjects.notes: External addon fragment, not mission SQF.

generatedMissionRules.postCopyPatch: Takistan SET_MAP is patched from 1 to 2.
generatedMissionRules.moddedGeneration: Disabled/commented in LoadoutManager; treat Modded_Missions/* as stale/non-authoritative.
generatedMissionRules.packaging: ZipManager zips Missions and Missions_Vanilla only, then writes _MISSIONS.7z.
